schema_notes: Per-billionaire record following Ellison v3 reference. Every dollar has source_url + retrieved_at. Events canonically scoped by event_role. Classification for Tier A/B/C happens downstream, not in this file.
generated_by: scrooge v3 manual reference run — single-subject deep research (Richard Kinder) + regen_v3@sha256-30cea1997160dc80 + regen_v3@sha256-67b34abc3afc4998 + regen_v3@sha256-f0a9361eb7f330b4

person.wealth_source: Co-founder and Executive Chairman of Kinder Morgan Inc. (KMI) — North America's largest energy infrastructure / pipeline operator. Majority of fortune from ~12% stake in KMI. Co-founded with William Morgan in 1997 after leaving Enron as president/COO.
person.spouse: Nancy G. Kinder (co-philanthropist; President/CEO of Kinder Foundation)

net_worth.range_note: Forbes 2026 World's Billionaires List places Kinder at $13B, rank ~232 globally (Houston's richest). Bloomberg's 2024-2025 real-time index tracked lower ~$10.6-11.2B. Fortune Oct 2025 cites $11.2B. Dollar-range tight vs. Ellison; pipeline stake is less volatile than Oracle.

sources.publisher: Forbes (via CultureMap Houston / InnovationMap reporting on 2026 World's Billionaires List)

net_worth.liquidity_notes: Kinder's wealth is ~12% stake in Kinder Morgan (public, NYSE:KMI). Less concentrated than Ellison's 41% Oracle stake; stake is smaller relative to float, so divesting would move stock less. Kinder also historically takes $1 salary and collects his compensation primarily via dividends — large, predictable cash flow. Apply 55% liquidity estimate (vs. Ellison's 25%).

cited_events.recipient: Houston-area charities (parks, arts, education, quality of life)
cited_events.notes: Oct 2025 announcement at Emancipation Park groundbreaking: Kinders committed $10B (95% of then-$11.2B net worth) to Houston charities, with bulk expected to pass via Kinder Foundation bequest. Reaffirmation of 2011 Giving Pledge with explicit geographic focus.

pledges_and_announcements.status_source: Kinder Foundation public reporting: >$850M committed to Houston projects as of Dec 2024; reaffirmed in Oct 2025 $10B Houston-specific pledge.
pledge_letter_key_quotes: 95% of their wealth to charity either during their lifetimes or at their deaths (exceeded the Giving Pledge's 50% minimum)
Focus on three areas: education, urban green space, and quality of life — concentrated on Houston
pledges_and_announcements.notes: RESEARCH BRIEF CORRECTION: User prompt cited 2014 signing; actual year is 2011 per Giving Pledge website and multiple independent sources. Wealth at 2011 signing ~$5B (pre-Kinder Morgan IPO consolidation). 95% target at signing ~$4.75B. Current net worth $13B; 95% target now ~$12.35B. Gap between observable foundation giving (~$850M through 2024) and 95% target is ~$11.5B — but pledge is lifetime-OR-estate, so bulk can land at death via Kinder Foundation bequest.

political_giving.substitution_note: Not counted as philanthropy. NBER W26616 estimates political giving displaces charitable giving at ~$0.33 per $1. Kinder's political giving is modest relative to his foundation giving.
political_giving.observed_total_note: OpenSecrets profile exists for 'Kinder, Richard D. & Nancy G.' (donor ID U0000004227). Full multi-cycle FEC API pull not yet run. Known contributions are oil-industry–aligned Republican committees/candidates; total appears to be low-single-digit millions across 2012-2024 cycles — an order of magnitude smaller than Ellison's $31M+. Kinder Morgan corporate PAC separately reported $167,757 in 2024 cycle (not Richard's personal giving).

sources.publisher: OpenSecrets — donor detail for Richard & Nancy Kinder (personal)
OpenSecrets — Kinder Morgan Inc corporate PAC (NOT personal)

foundations_active.name: Kinder Foundation
foundations_active.officers_compensation: Chairman Richard D. Kinder and President/CEO Nancy G. Kinder receive ZERO compensation. Top paid officers are operational staff: Guy Hagstette (Sr. VP Parks & Civic Projects, $398,622), Sarah Newbery (Director of Parks & Greenspace, $260,886), Patra Brannon (Director of Education & Community Projects, $225,561). These are reasonable salaries for a foundation running parks projects.
foundations_active.payout_rate_notes: 2024 grants-out ($59.9M) vs assets ($173.4M) = 34.5% — well above 5% legal minimum. Revenue ($84.5M) exceeds expenses ($61.3M) — foundation is being continuously fed by the Kinders. Cumulative commitments >$850M since 1997 per Foundation's own reporting.
foundations_active.red_flag_daf_transfers: NOT DETECTED — Kinder Foundation grants flow to specific named Houston institutions (Rice, MFAH, MD Anderson, Buffalo Bayou Partnership, Memorial Park Conservancy, Houston Parks Board). No DAF intermediary pattern observed.

detected_vehicles.dafs_note: No DAF activity identified. Kinders give via their named foundation direct to recipient institutions.
detected_vehicles.llcs_note: No philanthropic LLC detected. Giving is 501(c)(3) foundation structure.
detected_vehicles.for_profit_hybrid_note: No for-profit hybrid vehicles detected. Unlike Ellison's EIT pivot, Kinder's philanthropy is traditional 501(c)(3) grant-making.
detected_vehicles.offshore_note: ICIJ Offshore Leaks name-match NOT YET RUN — deferred to Phase 2. No public reporting so far.

red_flags_not_present: LOW_PAYOUT (Kinder Foundation 2024 payout 34.5% of assets; cumulative 30+ years of active grant-making)
HIGH_OFFICER_COMP (Kinders take zero comp; operational staff paid reasonable market rates for Houston foundation work)
DAF_TRANSFERS (no DAF activity; grants flow direct to named Houston institutions)
PLEDGE_UNFULFILLED (observable giving $850M+ is ~7% of current net worth, >17% of wealth at 2011 signing; on-track at cohort median for living pledgers)
POLITICAL_DOMINATES_CHARITABLE (political giving is low single-digit millions vs. $850M+ foundation giving; ratio is ~100:1 charitable:political, opposite of Ellison)
NET_WORTH_SURGE_WITHOUT_GIVING_MATCH (giving has scaled with net worth: $31.5M in 2015 → $59.9M in 2024, ~2x over a decade; net worth grew ~2.5x in same period — reasonable match)

rollup.observable_giving_method: Kinder Foundation's own reporting of >$850M committed to Houston projects as of Dec 2024 (foundation website). Independently corroborated by sum(990-PF grants_out 2015-2024 known years) + pre-2015 disbursements (estimated from cumulative-minus-post-2015 gap). Press-cited cumulative figure climbed to >$900M by Oct 2025 after MD Anderson $150M and continued annual grants. Excludes: political giving; excludes 2025 $150M MD Anderson announcement (will hit 2025 990-PF, not yet filed); excludes 2026 $55M Rice announcement (not yet disbursed).
observable_giving_todo: Pull Kinder Foundation 990-PFs for years 2016-2018 from ProPublica (gap in current data pull).
Update when 2025 and 2026 990-PFs file (will capture MD Anderson $150M and Rice $55M).
Run FEC bulk pull on Richard + Nancy Kinder for exact multi-cycle political total.
rollup.expected_giving_method: 10% × liquid_wealth × min(years_as_bil/10, 1). liquid_wealth = 0.55 × $13B = $7.15B. Tenure cap saturated (20 years). Expected = $715M.

hidden_upper_usd.offshore_uplift_note: Not yet checked. Zero until checked.
hidden_upper_usd.anonymous_cohort_method: Kinders are explicitly public about their giving (press conferences, named buildings, foundation website disclosure). Anonymous cohort uplift should be MINIMAL. Applied floor: 0.05% × liquid_wealth = $3.6M; rounded up to $50M as upper bound to cover small unreported personal gifts outside foundation.
hidden_upper_usd.religious_note: No publicly disclosed religious giving pattern.
hidden_upper_usd.estate_bequest_method: The Kinders' 2011 Giving Pledge + 2025 reaffirmation explicitly commits 95% of wealth, with the portion not given during lifetime to pass to Kinder Foundation at death. At current $13B, 95% = $12.35B; observable giving-so-far ~$0.86B; gap ~$11.5B. UNLIKE Ellison's trust (structure undisclosed, 'virtually all' vague), Kinder's bequest path is explicit and flows through an existing operating foundation with decades of grant-making history. Credibility of estate bequest is therefore HIGH. Listed here for confidence-interval visibility; do NOT include in tier calculation (bequest has not yet disbursed).
hidden_upper_usd.total_method: anonymous only. Estate bequest is a SEPARATE line in the confidence interval, not a hidden-giving uplift.

rollup.tier_rule: Observable giving ($858M) exceeds 10%-of-liquid-wealth expectation ($715M). Giving velocity is rising ($31M in 2015 to $60M in 2024). Zero red flags. Pledge signed 2011 and reaffirmed Oct 2025 with explicit $10B Houston commitment. Published tier: 'On Track' — Kinders are actively delivering on their pledge through a well-run operating foundation, with a credible estate-bequest path for the remainder. This is the canonical counter-example to Ellison: same Giving Pledge signers, dramatically different observable behavior.
rollup.tier_published_caveat: Readers should see Kinder as the canonical example of 'signed the Giving Pledge, actually doing it.' Observable giving exceeds the 10% formal norm. The remaining 93% of wealth is committed via estate bequest to an existing operating foundation — this is higher-credibility than opaque trust structures. Main uncertainty: whether lifetime vs. estate split is suboptimal (estate-only giving delays impact by decades). Counterfactual: if Kinder stopped giving today, Houston loses its largest private parks/arts/education funder.
rollup.expected_5pct_tenure_method: 5% × net_worth_best × liquidity_pct × years_as_billionaire (uncapped tenure); computed by aggregate_v3.py
rollup.observable_reconciliation_note: Published `observable_giving_usd` reflects agent judgment about attributable giving. `observable_from_events_usd` is the strict sum of canonical grant_out + direct_gift event amounts. A gap can mean self-reported lifetime figures beyond cited events (rollup > sum) or exclusion of inherited-foundation / family-foundation portions (rollup < sum).

scrooge_legacy_score_internal_only.note: Observable giving exceeds 10%-of-liquid-wealth expectation; no red flags; pledge on-track. All penalty components zero. Computed with v2 formula for continuity with current pipeline. DO NOT PUBLISH this number — we publish tiers only per PLAN.md.

right_of_reply.planned_outreach_route: Kinder Foundation communications contact via kinderfoundation.org; cc Nancy Kinder (President/CEO). Likely to respond favorably — they publish their own financials and welcome scrutiny.
